title: sindora galedupa
intro: Sindora galedupa, also known as Sindora galedupa, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant's leaves and seeds are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, providing health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating cough, and soothing sore throats. Sindora galedupa can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and fever-related conditions.
